title: Locking Support on Sybase

Locking Support on Sybase

Locking Support on Sybase

Uniface provides optimistic and paranoid locking. Optimistic locking is used except when the read/lock instruction is issued. All locking is performed by the Sybase DBMS.

Default Locking Type

The SYB connector accesses Sybase's automatic optimistic locking at all times, unless overridden by the read/lock instruction. The ProcScript lock instruction is ignored for Sybase. read/lock is the only way of overriding optimistic locking in Sybase.

Deadlock Detection

The SYB connector uses the Sybase error handling mechanism to detect deadlock. Upon deadlock, the connector raises an error in its internal error handling mechanism. When a logon path is marked as deadlocked, no work is allowed on the path until a commit or rollback instruction is issued.

The connector blocks the logon path so that the user cannot be misled into believing that additional work is being performed on the deadlocked transaction. All new work would be performed in a new transaction.

read/lock and holdlock

The read/lock instruction creates a Sybase holdlock. If a second user tries to write to a record which has a holdlock applied to it, then the operation fails and the following happens:

  • Sybase waits for the blocking lock to be released; there is no time-out.
  • If the lock is not released, a deadlock develops and the transaction fails for the user with the least CPU time in their transaction.

Normally, Uniface sets $status to -10 if a user tries to modify and store a record which has been locked by another user, but because Sybase does not return a status in this situation, Uniface is unable to set $status to -10.

Note: Using the read/lock instruction immediately forces a holdlock in Sybase, which prevents all other users from writing to the record concerned (or to any other record in the locked page); this does not prevent users from reading these records.

Uniface uses the holdlock option to enforce data integrity. A holdlock is issued if a user modifies a record, and the record is then updated. Another user cannot update the same record while the holdlock is in force. Without the holdlock, the second user’s data would update the record and be immediately overwritten by the first user’s data, which destroys data integrity. When a holdlock is used, the lock is held until the current transaction is ended.

U_VERSION

It is recommended that you use U_VERSION, as this significantly reduces the chance of lock conflicts.

Isolation Level

The Sybase DBMS provides ANSI-compatible isolation level controls which users can configure via the lock level connector option.

Related Links
  • U_VERSION for Improving Locking Performance

    Uniface supports a version mechanism which can improve performance by using a field called U_VERSION. U_VERSION is a one-byte field which you must assign to an entity for it to work. In other words, it is not automatically available.
